description: Layered bidirectional text terminology mapping for Claude <-> Codex instruction and skill sync. Users may edit this file or provide a project/local override with the same shape. Model vocabulary is NOT defined here — it is managed in rules/agents-map.json (single source of truth) and merged in at runtime.

layers.description: Host-specific config and instruction file names.

rules.description: Translate Claude grouped agent files (.claude/agents/<group>/<name>.md) to flat Codex agent files (.codex/agents/<name>.toml). Codex agents are flat — no subdirectories — and the engine's entryRulePaths already drops the group when locating the codex file (item.split('/').pop()), so this rule mirrors that behavior: keep only <name>.toml on the codex side. Listed before agent-file-path so this longer match wins. Reverse direction is intentionally inert (codex_pattern = (?!) matches nothing) because the codex side carries no group information to reconstruct; the flat agent-file-path rule handles codex->claude.

Translate flat agent file paths and extensions (.claude/agents/<name>.md <-> .codex/agents/<name>.toml). The capture group accepts mustache placeholders like {name} so prose patterns ('~/.claude/agents/{name}.md') translate too. Slashes are excluded from the name capture so subdirectory paths fall through to agent-file-path-grouped above.

Translate skill directory roots (.claude/skills/<name> <-> .agents/skills/<name>). Codex's canonical skills directory is .agents/skills (matches globalPaths().codex.skills first candidate); .codex/skills is a less-common alternative handled at the filesystem enumeration layer rather than here.
Translate skill manifest filename references in body text. Claude loads skill.md (lowercase); Codex loads SKILL.md (uppercase). Word boundaries keep the rule from firing inside unrelated tokens.

Generic catchall for any .claude/<rest> directory reference. Listed last in this layer so the more specific rules above (agent-file-path with .md<->.toml, skill-file-path with .claude/skills <-> .agents/skills, skill-manifest-filename) match first; this rule then catches everything else (.claude/docs, .claude/hooks, .claude/insights, .claude/insight, .claude/plugins, etc.) with a plain prefix swap to .codex/<rest>. Negative lookaheads carve out the literal exception files handled by the literal rules above (settings.json/mcp.json on the Claude side, config.toml on the Codex side) so those keep their precise filename mapping (settings.json <-> config.toml, mcp.json <-> config.toml [mcp_servers]) instead of getting a partial prefix-only swap.

layers.description: Generic agent delegation and reasoning vocabulary. Workflow-specific intents live in host-target-templates.json.

rules.description: Translate prose mentions of the Claude SDK TeamCreate / TeamDelete tool name to Codex's spawn_agent fan-out wording. Negative lookbehind prevents matching inside `ai-config-sync:*` marker JSON (where call names are preserved verbatim for round-trip restore); negative lookahead prevents matching the bare-call form `TeamCreate(...)` (which is handled by rules/call-templates.json). Reverse direction restores from the canonical 'multiple spawn_agent invocations' phrasing back to TeamCreate.

Translate prose mentions of the Claude SDK TaskCreate / TaskUpdate / TaskGet / TaskList / TaskOutput / TaskStop tool names to spawn_agent. Codex has no native task tracker; in instructional text these names always describe sub-agent dispatch and the Codex equivalent is spawn_agent. Negative lookbehind avoids rewriting call names captured inside `ai-config-sync:*` marker JSON; negative lookahead skips the bare-call form `TaskCreate(...)` (handled by rules/call-templates.json). The reverse direction is intentionally inert here: 'spawn_agent' has its own canonical reverse via the task-delegation rule (-> 'Task tool').
Translate prose mentions of the Claude SDK SendMessage tool to Codex's send_input. Same lookbehind/lookahead guards as the Task and Team rules.

layers.description: Permission and sandbox vocabulary used in text instructions.
Host CLI invocation vocabulary used in text instructions (headless / non-interactive forms).

rules.description: Translate the headless invocation form: `claude -p` or `claude --print` (Claude CLI print mode) <-> `codex exec` or `codex e` (Codex non-interactive subcommand). Listed before bare 'claude' / 'codex' references so the longer form wins.

layers.description: Hook, MCP, and web access vocabulary used in text instructions.
